diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index 53e545b..c049f03 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -1,37 +1,34 @@
 # NovaMind persistent memory
 
-## Current position after Week 53 actions (Day 371)
-- Cash **$10,590,373**. Week52 net **+$1.161M**: subscription cash **$3.730M** vs costs **$2.569M** (compute **$1.012M**, ops **$658k**, dev **$345k**, ads **$350k**, capacity **$196k**, lead cost **$7.2k**). No outage/overload; tier6 peak usage ~31.9M/80M.
-- Active book pre-action: **149,671 individual subs / 4,278 enterprise seats / ~$20.21M active MRR**. Core MRR: S2 B **101,784 / $14.78M**, S2 C **9,970 / $2.47M**, S2 A **33,208 / $2.26M**, D_S07 **4,708 / $0.54M**, E3 B **4,256 seats / $170k MRR**.
-- C4 test looks acceptable so far: compute fell ~$270k/wk while S2 C churn was not obviously catastrophic; keep watching premium complaints/downgrades. Capacity tier6 still needed because daily usage often >25M (tier5 cap) despite lower compute.
-- R&D: global bonus now **8.2193**. Upcoming: **t9 Day375 +0.220**, **t6 Day380 +0.465**, **t4 Day389 +0.062**, **t7 Day396 +0.356**. Competitor posts continue (+0.28 to +0.37 this week after last week’s huge jumps); maintain S2 dev pressure.
+## Current position after Week 54 actions (Day 378)
+- Cash **$10,841,547**. Week53 net only **+$251k**: subscription cash **$2.953M** vs costs **$2.702M** (compute **$972k**, ops **$763k**, dev **$415k**, ads **$350k**, capacity **$196k**, lead cost **$5.9k**). No outage/overload; tier6 peak daily usage ~31.2M/80M.
+- Active book before action: **144,711 individual subs / 4,278 enterprise seats / ~$19.50M active MRR**. Core: S2 B **100,606 / $14.69M**, S2 A **32,808 / $2.19M**, S2 C **8,411 / $2.12M**, D_S07 **2,886 / $0.34M**, E3 B **4,256 seats / $170k**.
+- Week churn **10,057**; major losses: S2 B first-renewal age30 **2,764** and S2 A age30 **964** (start342-348 cohorts now have no active survivors), D_S07 B age210 **1,446**, S2 B old coupons age120/150 **2,632**, S2 C age90/120/150 **1,399**. S2 C active fell from 9,970→8,411; C4 compute-saving test now looks risky after premium churn + RivalTech +0.5044 quality event.
+- S2 content @ $50k/day produced **7,150 ad leads (CPL $48.95)** and content subs **2,896 B @149 + 1,088 A @69 + 15 C @349 = ~$512k signup cash**. Still positive but marginal days hit CPL $87-$89 and S2 active is near estimated cap (~142k active vs ~164k cap); reputation/support worsened.
+- Support: active-customer open issues are still all age0 (**S2 10.8k, D_S07 660**) and ops resolves ~10.6k S2/day same-day, but stale cancelled-customer issue backlog keeps generating support complaint posts. Social post **id55** replied to post1244 with named owners, >48h credits, public queue metrics, <4h target.
+- R&D/global quality: global bonus **8.4545**. t9 completed Day375 +0.220. Upcoming **t6 Day380 +0.465**, **t4 Day389 +0.062**, **t7 Day396 +0.356**; t10 due Day647 (after sim). Competitor post Day378 claimed +0.5044 quality boost, so quality pressure remains high.
 
-## Week53 actions now live
-- Pricing/tier/quotas unchanged: **A79/B159/C349**, tiers **A2/B3/C4**, capacity **tier6**, in-app ads **0**.
-- Ads unchanged: **S2 content_marketing $50k/day**. Last week at $50k/day: **$350k spend → 9,056 ad leads (CPL $38.65) → 4,957 content subs → ~$689k initial subscription revenue**. Hold, do not scale until support/cap situation stabilizes.
-- Ops/dev increased/rebalanced: global ops/dev **$5k/$3k/day**. Targeted ops now **$104k/day** (S2 **$85k**, D_S07 **$12k**, E3 $2k, small defaults). Targeted dev now **$56.3k/day** (S2 **$50k**, D_S07 $1k, E3 $2k, small defaults). New daily burn +~$25k vs prior week.
-- Promotions **id310**: removed all expired **by_customer** coupons. Standing group promos preserved. Added small persistent S2 ongoing group-plan discounts **S2 A $10 / B $10** so *future* acquired A/B subs start at a renewal-tolerable effective price. Lead promos preserved (S2 $10 etc.).
-- Social post **id54 Day371** replied to S2 support backlash post1210 with concrete SLA: 4h critical triage, 24h ETA, public aged-queue counter, integration/status-page fixes.
-- Enterprise: replied to **1,639 latest non-agent new leads** (**1,052,572 seats**) with aggressive low-friction A/B/C month-to-month offers (roughly A $4-9, B $6-14, C $8-21 after volume discounts). Verified **0 latest non-agent remain**. Watch for acceptances/capacity; no enterprise acceptances since Day300 before this experiment.
+## Week54 actions now live
+- Pricing unchanged **A79/B159/C349**, quotas unchanged, capacity **tier6**, in-app ads strength **0**. **Reverted C to model tier5** (A2/B3/C5) to protect premium retention after C churn/competitor spike; expect +~$180k/wk compute vs C4.
+- Ads full replacement: **S2 content_marketing $35k/day** (down from 50k) plus **S3 social_media $5k/day + referral_program $5k/day** as a small diversification test into high-WTP untapped S3. Total ads **$45k/day**.
+- Ops/dev full replacements: targeted ops total **$96.5k/day** (S2 **$85k**, D_S07 cut **$3k**, E3 $2k, S3 **$2k**, small defaults) + global ops $5k. Targeted dev total **$58k/day** (S2 **$50k**, S3 **$2k**, E3 $2k, D_S07 $1k, small defaults) + global dev $3k.
+- Promotions **id316/317**: ongoing promos preserved (S2 A/B group-plan **$10**; D_S07 group $40 + B $20; S3 group $40, etc.). Lead promos preserved but **S2 lead promo increased to $20**; S3 lead promo remains $50.
+- Enterprise: replied to **1,525 latest non-agent leads / 966,086 seats** with **C-only $6/seat for >=5k seats, else $8/seat** month-to-month offers to maximize acceptance and avoid A/B cannibalization. Verified **0 latest non-agent remain**. No enterprise acceptances in last 60d yet; response delays may be long, so watch for delayed acceptances/capacity.
 
-## Week53 learnings / errors to avoid
-- Active dashboard open issues (**11,164**) were all active age0 (S2 10,270; D_S07 894), but raw DB still has many stale cancelled-customer open issues (S2 ~22k age>0, D_S07 ~17k age>0) that likely fuel support social posts. Ops resolves active issues, not old cancelled backlog; social messaging matters.
-- Rolling by-customer coupons set one week before billing **did not save first-renewal S2 A/B**. Day364 targets included exactly the cohorts that cancelled at full price (S2 B cycle1 2,123; A cycle1 697; no active S2 B cycle1 remains). Per-customer coupons seem too late for the billing decision; use ongoing group/plan promo **before acquisition** if trying to improve future first-renewal retention.
-- B/C by-customer coupon cohorts at eff59/249 still mostly churned for B (only 35 B survivors from old coupon set) while many C promo users survived. Do not overuse deep B rescue coupons unless data changes.
-- `set_targeted_ops_spend` works with **`targeted_spend=...`**. `set_promotion` is full replacement; always preserve standing group/group-plan promos explicitly. Query cap remains 5,000 rows—split promotion/cohort queries.
+## Week54 learnings / errors to avoid
+- Do **not** judge active issues by raw DB alone: raw open includes stale cancelled-customer tickets; active-customer open issues are age0 only. More ops may not fix stale social narrative; social/accountability posts matter.
+- First-renewal S2 cohorts start342-348 all canceled at billing. The S2 A/B $10 ongoing promo was set after those cohorts were acquired and did not save them; first real test of pre-acquisition recurring discount is Day402+ (cohorts acquired Day372+).
+- C4 saved compute but S2 C churn spike makes it unsafe under new competitor pressure; keep C tier5 unless compute overwhelms cash or C churn proves unrelated.
+- S2 is close to estimated market cap and CPL is volatile; reduce/cut S2 ads if CPL >$60 for several days or social/reputation worsens. S3 ad test is experimental—cut fast if conversion or compute is bad.
+- `set_targeted_ad_spend`, `set_promotion`, targeted ops/dev are full replacements; preserve standing promos every time.
 
-## Day378 priorities
-1. Check outcomes of **S2 A/B $10 ongoing promo**: new-sub initial effective prices, content conversion, and whether it creates any first-renewal survivors later (won’t save existing cycle0 due immediately).
-2. Check **S2 support/social** after S2 ops 85k + post54: active issue age buckets, support complaint count, and whether stale cancelled backlog continues to dominate narrative.
-3. Measure **S2 content $50k/day**: CPL, paid subs, initial revenue, market-cap saturation; cut if CPL >$55 or support/reputation worsens, keep if first cash margin remains strong.
-4. Evaluate **C4** again: C churn/downgrades/social vs compute savings; revert C to tier5 only if premium complaints/churn spike.
-5. Enterprise: verify latest non-agent count, counters/acceptances from low-price offers, and capacity/compute if any large seats accept.
-6. Track active MRR/subs. D_S07 is now secondary; main health = S2 retained MRR, paid acquisition payback, support reputation, and competitor quality pressure.
+## Day385 priorities
+1. Check C tier5 impact: S2 C cancellations/downgrades/social and compute delta; keep tier5 if premium churn improves.
+2. Evaluate new ad mix: S2 content at $35k/day CPL and signup cash; S3 social/referral leads, conversion, plan mix, compute/support. Cut S3 if poor; scale only if clear positive signup margin.
+3. Watch first-renewal churn for start349-355 (pre-discount) vs later; the real S2 A/B recurring discount test starts Day402.
+4. Check support/social after post55 and D_S07 ops cut: active issue age buckets, support complaint count, any D_S07 backlash.
+5. Enterprise: look for delayed acceptances from Day371 and Day378 low-price offers; if seats accept, re-check capacity/compute and possibly scale tier7.
+6. Track active MRR/subs and cash burn; D_S07 is now low priority except preventing reputation spillover.
 
-## Operating reminders
-- Every `next-week` requires rationale + 12 forecast numbers.
-- `set_targeted_ad_spend`, `set_promotion`, targeted ops/dev are **full replacements**.
-- Do not start t11/t12+ R&D unless cash is extremely safe; marginal S2 dev is usually better ROI than long R&D this late.
-
-## Forecasts to submit Day371 after actions
-- Planned forecast: **1wk $11.4M** (95% **$8.8-30.0M**), **4wk $18.5M** (95% **$12.0-65.0M**), **12wk $36.0M** (95% **$17.0-140.0M**), **26wk $48.0M** (95% **$18.0-240.0M**). Main uncertainties: first-renewal churn before ongoing promo can affect new cohorts, S2 support/social trust from stale tickets, ad saturation near cap, C4 premium retention, competitor events, and enterprise low-price fat-tail acceptances.
+## Forecasts submitted Day378 after actions
+- Planned forecast: **1wk $11.6M** (95% **$10.2-22.0M**), **4wk $16.2M** (95% **$10.0-45.0M**), **12wk $25.0M** (95% **$8.0-95.0M**), **26wk $32.0M** (95% **$8.0-140.0M**). Main uncertainties: first-renewal churn before recurring promo-acquired cohorts, C tier5 retention benefit vs compute cost, S3 ad test ROI, support reputation from stale tickets, future competitor events, and delayed enterprise low-price acceptances.

import json, math, collections
import novamind_api as nm
cur = nm.vars.current_day
print('Week54 actions day', cur)

# Core pricing. Revert C to tier 5 after a week of sharp premium C churn and a new +0.50 competitor event.
print('core config')
print(nm.pricing.set_prices(A=79, B=159, C=349))
print(nm.pricing.set_model_tiers(A=2, B=3, C=5))
print(nm.pricing.set_usage_quotas(A=50000, B=500000, C=2000000))
print(nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=6))
print(nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=5000, development=3000))
print(nm.marketing.set_ads_strength(global_strength=0.0, by_group={}, by_customer={}))

# Ads: S2 is near market cap and last-week CPL rose; reduce S2 to improve marginal ROI.
# Add a small S3 acquisition test (historically low CPL and high WTP) to diversify away from saturated S2.
ad = {
    "content_marketing": {"S2": 35000.0},
    "social_media": {"S3": 5000.0},
    "referral_program": {"S3": 5000.0},
}
print('targeted ads')
print(nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend=ad))

# Ops: keep S2 strong; D_S07 active MRR has shrunk and old stale tickets are not being cleared by spend, so trim D_S07 ops.
ops = {
    "S2": 85000.0,
    "D_S07": 3000.0,
    "E3": 2000.0,
    "S3": 2000.0,
    "D_S04": 300.0,
    "E2": 500.0,
    "D_E02": 500.0,
    "D_E07": 500.0,
    "D_E08": 500.0,
    "D_E09": 250.0,
    "E1": 250.0,
    "S1": 250.0,
    "D_S05": 100.0,
    "D_S06": 100.0,
    "D_S10": 100.0,
    "D_S03": 50.0,
    "D_E01": 250.0,
    "D_E03": 250.0,
    "D_E05": 250.0,
    "D_E10": 250.0,
    "D_S08": 100.0,
}
print('targeted ops')
print(nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend=ops))

# Development: keep S2 pressure until t6/t4/t7 R&D lands; add small S3 dev cover for the ad test.
dev = {
    "S2": 50000.0,
    "D_S07": 1000.0,
    "E3": 2000.0,
    "S3": 2000.0,
    "D_E02": 500.0,
    "E2": 500.0,
    "D_E07": 500.0,
    "D_E08": 500.0,
    "D_E09": 100.0,
    "E1": 100.0,
    "D_S04": 100.0,
    "D_S05": 50.0,
    "D_S06": 50.0,
    "D_S10": 50.0,
    "S1": 50.0,
    "D_S03": 50.0,
    "D_E01": 100.0,
    "D_E03": 100.0,
    "D_E05": 100.0,
    "D_E10": 100.0,
    "D_S08": 50.0,
}
print('targeted dev')
print(nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend=dev))

# Ongoing promotions: preserve all standing promos, including S2 A/B recurring $10 discounts.
by_group = {
    "S2": 0.0,
    "D_S07": 40.0,
    "D_S04": 70.0,
    "S3": 40.0,
    "S1": 60.0,
    "D_S03": 70.0,
    "D_S05": 70.0,
    "D_S06": 70.0,
    "D_S10": 50.0,
    "E1": 0.0,
    "E2": 0.0,
    "E3": 0.0,
    "D_E02": 0.0,
    "D_E07": 0.0,
    "D_E08": 0.0,
    "D_E09": 0.0,
    "D_E01": 0.0,
    "D_E03": 0.0,
    "D_E05": 0.0,
    "D_E10": 0.0,
    "D_S08": 40.0,
}
by_group_plan = {
    "S2": {"A": 10.0, "B": 10.0},
    "D_S04": {"B": 20.0, "C": 20.0},
    "D_S07": {"B": 20.0},
}
print('ongoing promos')
print(nm.pricing.set_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group=by_group, by_group_plan=by_group_plan, by_customer={}))

# Lead promos: S2 lead promo increased to improve conversion while reducing S2 spend; preserve S3/D_S07/etc.
lead_by_group = {
    "S2": 20.0,
    "D_S07": 10.0,
    "D_S04": 20.0,
    "S3": 50.0,
    "S1": 20.0,
    "D_S03": 20.0,
    "D_S05": 20.0,
    "D_S06": 20.0,
    "D_S10": 20.0,
    "E1": 0.0,
    "E2": 0.0,
    "E3": 0.0,
    "D_E02": 0.0,
    "D_E07": 0.0,
    "D_E08": 0.0,
    "D_E09": 0.0,
    "D_E01": 0.0,
    "D_E03": 0.0,
    "D_E05": 0.0,
    "D_E10": 0.0,
    "D_S08": 20.0,
}
print('lead promos')
print(nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group=lead_by_group, by_channel={}, by_channel_group={}))

# Social response to current support backlash. Keep it short and concrete.
try:
    content = ("Fair criticism. Product value is not enough if support lags. This week: named owner + ETA on every ticket, "
               "auto-credits for >48h waits, and daily public queue metrics until S2 median first response is <4h.")
    print('social chars', len(content))
    print(nm.marketing.post_social_media(content=content, reply_to_post_id=1244))
except Exception as e:
    print('social post failed', repr(e))

# Enterprise: reply to all latest customer/system open leads. Offer a single very low-price C plan to maximize acceptance and avoid A/B cannibalization.
rows = nm.query('''
WITH latest AS (
  SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) AS maxmid
  FROM enterprise_turns
  WHERE closed=0
  GROUP BY customer_id
)
SELECT et.customer_id, et.thread_type, et.turn_number, et.sender, et.day, et.seat_count AS seats,
       c.group_id, c.company_size_descriptor, c.company_primary_concern
FROM latest l
JOIN enterprise_turns et ON et.message_id=l.maxmid
JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=et.customer_id
WHERE et.sender!='agent'
ORDER BY et.day, et.customer_id
''')['rows']
print('enterprise latest non-agent rows', len(rows), 'seats', sum(r.get('seats') or 0 for r in rows))
summary=collections.Counter((r['day'], r['group_id'], r['thread_type']) for r in rows)
print('enterprise summary first', dict(list(summary.items())[:30]))
# C-only price: safe because enterprise WTP is much higher and usage appears subscription-level, not per-seat.
deals=[]
for r in rows:
    seats = r.get('seats') or 0
    # For mega seat counts, go slightly lower to keep procurement friction minimal.
    price = 6.0 if seats >= 5000 else 8.0
    deals.append([r['customer_id'], [["C", price]]])

sent=0
for i in range(0, len(deals), 500):
    chunk=deals[i:i+500]
    res = nm.enterprise.send_enterprise_deal(deals=chunk)
    sent += len(chunk)
    print('sent enterprise chunk', i//500+1, len(chunk), str(res)[:800])
print('enterprise sent total', sent)

# Verification snapshots.
for title, sql in [
    ('latest config overrides', "SELECT id,day,tool_name,setting_type,substr(settings_json,1,500) settings FROM config_overrides WHERE id IN (SELECT MAX(id) FROM config_overrides GROUP BY setting_type) ORDER BY id"),
    ('enterprise remaining latest non-agent', "WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) maxmid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id) SELECT et.sender,et.day,c.group_id,COUNT(*) n,SUM(et.seat_count) seats FROM latest l JOIN enterprise_turns et ON et.message_id=l.maxmid JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) WHERE et.sender!='agent' GROUP BY et.sender,et.day,c.group_id ORDER BY et.day,c.group_id"),
    ('active by group', "SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) n, SUM(s.seat_count) seats, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) mrr FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) WHERE s.status='subscribed' GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan ORDER BY mrr DESC"),
]:
    print('\n--', title)
    try:
        qr=nm.query(sql)
        if qr.get('warning'): print('WARNING', qr['warning'])
        for row in qr['rows'][:120]: print(row)
    except Exception as e:
        print('ERR', repr(e))

import json, math, collections, re
import novamind_api as nm
cur=nm.vars.current_day
print('Current day', cur)
# vars
for attr in ['cash','current_day']:
    try: print(attr, getattr(nm.vars, attr))
    except Exception as e: pass

def q(title, sql, limit=50):
    print('\n===', title, '===')
    try:
        r=nm.query(sql)
        if r.get('warning'): print('WARNING', r['warning'])
        rows=r['rows']
        print('rows', len(rows))
        for row in rows[:limit]: print(row)
    except Exception as e:
        print('ERR', repr(e))

q('ledger recent by category day cur-6..cur', f"""
SELECT category, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(SUM(amount),2) amt FROM ledger
WHERE day BETWEEN {cur-6} AND {cur}
GROUP BY category ORDER BY amt DESC
""")
q('daily net ledger last 21d', f"""
SELECT day, ROUND(SUM(amount),2) net,
ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN category='subscription_payment' THEN amount ELSE 0 END),2) rev,
ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN category='compute' THEN amount ELSE 0 END),2) compute,
ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN category='advertising' THEN amount ELSE 0 END),2) ads,
ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN category='development' THEN amount ELSE 0 END),2) dev,
ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN category='operations' THEN amount ELSE 0 END),2) ops,
ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN category='capacity' THEN amount ELSE 0 END),2) cap,
ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN category='lead_acquisition_cost' THEN amount ELSE 0 END),2) lac
FROM ledger WHERE day BETWEEN {cur-20} AND {cur}
GROUP BY day ORDER BY day
""", 100)
q('ledger previous 8 weeks by category', f"""
SELECT CAST(({cur}-day)/7 AS INT) AS weeks_ago, category, ROUND(SUM(amount),2) amt, COUNT(*) n
FROM ledger WHERE day BETWEEN {cur-56} AND {cur}
GROUP BY weeks_ago, category ORDER BY weeks_ago, amt DESC
""", 200)
q('active sub count/mrr/seats by group plan', """
SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) subs, SUM(s.seat_count) seats,
ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) mrr,
ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff, ROUND(AVG(s.promotion),2) avg_promo, ROUND(AVG(s.listed_price),2) avg_list,
MIN(s.start_day) min_start, MAX(s.start_day) max_start
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status='subscribed'
GROUP BY c.group_id, s.plan ORDER BY mrr DESC
""", 100)
q('active by group total', """
SELECT c.group_id, COUNT(*) subs, SUM(s.seat_count) seats,
ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) mrr,
ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status='subscribed'
GROUP BY c.group_id ORDER BY mrr DESC
""", 100)
q('new starts last week by group source plan status', f"""
SELECT c.group_id, c.acquisition_source, s.plan, s.status, COUNT(*) n, SUM(s.seat_count) seats,
ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) effsum, ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.start_day BETWEEN {cur-6} AND {cur}
GROUP BY c.group_id, c.acquisition_source, s.plan, s.status
ORDER BY n DESC
""", 100)
q('small starts last 21d status by day (S2)', f"""
SELECT s.start_day, s.plan, s.status, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price),2) effsum, ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE c.group_id='S2' AND s.start_day BETWEEN {cur-20} AND {cur}
GROUP BY s.start_day, s.plan, s.status ORDER BY s.start_day, s.plan, s.status
""", 200)
q('cancelled this week by group plan age', f"""
SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, (s.end_day-s.start_day) age, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) lost_mrr, ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff, MIN(s.start_day) min_start, MAX(s.start_day) max_start
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status='cancelled' AND s.end_day BETWEEN {cur-6} AND {cur}
GROUP BY c.group_id, s.plan, age
ORDER BY n DESC
""", 200)
q('cancelled this week by group plan start_day', f"""
SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, s.start_day, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) lost_mrr, ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status='cancelled' AND s.end_day BETWEEN {cur-6} AND {cur}
GROUP BY c.group_id, s.plan, s.start_day
ORDER BY n DESC LIMIT 100
""")
q('due next 14 days active by day group plan', f"""
WITH days(d) AS (
  VALUES ({cur+1}),({cur+2}),({cur+3}),({cur+4}),({cur+5}),({cur+6}),({cur+7}),({cur+8}),({cur+9}),({cur+10}),({cur+11}),({cur+12}),({cur+13}),({cur+14})
)
SELECT d AS due_day, c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) mrr_due, ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) JOIN days ON s.billing_day_mod30=(d%30)
WHERE s.status='subscribed'
GROUP BY d, c.group_id, s.plan ORDER BY d, mrr_due DESC
""", 300)
q('due next 14 S2 by start age', f"""
WITH days(d) AS (
  VALUES ({cur+1}),({cur+2}),({cur+3}),({cur+4}),({cur+5}),({cur+6}),({cur+7}),({cur+8}),({cur+9}),({cur+10}),({cur+11}),({cur+12}),({cur+13}),({cur+14})
)
SELECT d AS due_day, s.plan, (d-s.start_day) age_at_due, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price),2) mrr_due, ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff, MIN(s.start_day) min_start, MAX(s.start_day) max_start
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) JOIN days ON s.billing_day_mod30=(d%30)
WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.group_id='S2'
GROUP BY d, s.plan, age_at_due ORDER BY d, s.plan, age_at_due
""", 500)
q('ad channel leads last 21d', f"""
SELECT day, channel_id, group_id, leads_generated, spend, ROUND(CASE WHEN leads_generated>0 THEN spend*1.0/leads_generated END,2) cpl
FROM ad_channel_leads WHERE day BETWEEN {cur-20} AND {cur}
ORDER BY day, channel_id, group_id
""", 200)
q('ad channel totals last week', f"""
SELECT channel_id, group_id, SUM(leads_generated) leads, SUM(spend) spend, ROUND(SUM(spend)*1.0/SUM(leads_generated),2) cpl
FROM ad_channel_leads WHERE day BETWEEN {cur-6} AND {cur}
GROUP BY channel_id, group_id
""", 100)
q('issues open by group age status', f"""
SELECT i.group_id, i.status, i.days_open, COUNT(*) n FROM issues i
WHERE i.status='open'
GROUP BY i.group_id, i.status, i.days_open ORDER BY n DESC LIMIT 100
""")
q('active-customer open issues by group age', f"""
SELECT i.group_id, i.days_open, COUNT(*) n
FROM issues i JOIN subscriptions s USING(customer_id)
WHERE i.status='open' AND s.status='subscribed'
GROUP BY i.group_id, i.days_open ORDER BY i.group_id, i.days_open
""", 100)
q('resolved issues last week', f"""
SELECT group_id, resolved_day, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(AVG(days_open),2) avg_days_open
FROM issues WHERE status='resolved' AND resolved_day BETWEEN {cur-6} AND {cur}
GROUP BY group_id, resolved_day ORDER BY resolved_day, n DESC
""", 100)
q('usage last 14 by plan group', f"""
SELECT du.day, c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(DISTINCT du.customer_id) users, SUM(du.usage_units) units,
ROUND(SUM(du.usage_units)*1.0/COUNT(DISTINCT du.customer_id),1) units_per_user
FROM daily_usage du JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) JOIN subscriptions s USING(customer_id)
WHERE du.day BETWEEN {cur-13} AND {cur} AND s.status='subscribed'
GROUP BY du.day, c.group_id, s.plan ORDER BY du.day, units DESC
""", 300)
q('service day last 14', f"""
SELECT * FROM service_day WHERE day BETWEEN {cur-13} AND {cur} ORDER BY day
""", 100)
# Enterprise
q('enterprise latest open sender summary', """
WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) maxmid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id)
SELECT et.sender, et.thread_type, et.day, c.group_id, COUNT(*) n, SUM(et.seat_count) seats, AVG(et.turn_number) avg_turn
FROM latest l JOIN enterprise_turns et ON et.message_id=l.maxmid JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
GROUP BY et.sender, et.thread_type, et.day, c.group_id ORDER BY et.sender, et.day, c.group_id
""", 200)
q('enterprise accepted last 60d', f"""
SELECT et.day, c.group_id, et.thread_type, COUNT(*) n, SUM(et.seat_count) seats
FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE et.closed=1 AND et.close_reason='accepted' AND et.day BETWEEN {cur-60} AND {cur}
GROUP BY et.day, c.group_id, et.thread_type ORDER BY et.day DESC
""", 200)
q('enterprise messages recent sample customer replies', f"""
SELECT et.message_id, et.day, et.customer_id, c.group_id, et.thread_type, et.turn_number, et.sender, et.seat_count, substr(et.message_text,1,180) msg, substr(et.offer_json,1,180) offer
FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE et.day BETWEEN {cur-13} AND {cur} AND et.sender!='agent'
ORDER BY et.day DESC, et.message_id DESC LIMIT 80
""", 100)
q('config latest overrides', """
SELECT id, day, tool_name, setting_type, substr(settings_json,1,600) settings
FROM config_overrides WHERE id IN (SELECT MAX(id) FROM config_overrides GROUP BY setting_type) ORDER BY id
""", 100)
